source.title: Example Article on Bayesian Thinking
source.citation: Researcher, A., & Scholar, B. (2026). Example Article on Bayesian Thinking. Journal of Examples, 12(3), 1-18.

summary.plain: This article argues that Bayesian reasoning provides a coherent framework for scientific inference when prior assumptions are made explicit and updated transparently in light of data.
key_points: Bayesian inference combines prior information with observed data.
Model comparison should consider predictive performance.
Transparent assumptions are central to scientific interpretation.

quiz.title: Example Article on Bayesian Thinking quiz

questions.question: What is combined with observed data in Bayesian inference?
questions.explanation: Bayesian inference combines prior information with observed data.
